intro: Meta launched Muse Spark 1.1, an aggressively priced AI coding model that CEO Mark Zuckerberg says undercuts rivals' "extreme" pricing, sparking expectations of a broader price war in the competitive coding AI market. OpenAI released GPT-5.6 and a new ChatGPT Work AI agent designed to automate workplace tasks, intensifying competition with Microsoft, Anthropic, and Google for enterprise customers. In a copyright dispute with the New York Times and other news outlets, OpenAI faces potential sanctions after being accused of hiding evidence in a case that could reshape how AI companies license journalistic content.

whitelist.title: Meta's new AI model could spark a massive price war in the red-hot AI coding market
whitelist.desc: Meta launches a new AI coding model, Muse Spark 1.1, which is priced aggressively, CEO Mark Zuckerberg said. He called out rivals' "extreme" pricing.

whitelist.title: News outlets ask judge to sanction OpenAI in copyright case
whitelist.desc: The NYT, Daily News, and others accuse OpenAI of hiding evidence in their copyright suit, asking a judge for sanctions in a case that could reshape journalism.

whitelist.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work AI Agent
whitelist.desc: The new AI agent is designed to automate workplace tasks, escalating OpenAI’s battle with Microsoft, Anthropic and Google for enterprise customers.

whitelist.title: Meta to put its own AI chip into production in September, aiming to double computing capacity
whitelist.desc: Meta plans to put an in-house MTIA AI chip into production in September and roughly double its computing capacity, Reuters reports, deepening its push away from Nvidia.

whitelist.title: OpenAI releases a new ChatGPT tool for all things work related - Engadget
whitelist.desc: OpenAI's long-awaited super app is here.

whitelist.title: Behind The AI Gold Rush At RAISE Summit Paris
whitelist.desc: The RAISE Summit in Paris drew 9,000 attendees to discuss enterprise AI adoption, coding tools, and the burgeoning neocloud market. Data sovereignty was also stressed.

whitelist.title: ChatGPT can now finish what you started, and that’s a much bigger deal than it sounds

whitelist.title: Steven Bartlett stopped using AI to write LinkedIn posts. New data shows why that's a good idea.

whitelist.title: TSMC's Second Quarter Will Test Whether The AI Buildout Has A Ceiling
whitelist.desc: TSMC reports Q2 earnings July 16. Driven by the AI boom, investors eye guidance, 2026 capex, CoWoS advanced packaging expansion, and Arizona execution risks.

whitelist.title: United Nations Calls For An AI Child Safety Pledge
whitelist.desc: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for an AI Child Safety Pledge, initiating a global dialogue on AI governance to protect minors.

whitelist.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI

whitelist.title: Meta reportedly plans to start manufacturing its new AI chip in September.
whitelist.desc: The chip, codenamed “Iris,” will join the growing lineup of Meta Training and Inference Accelerators (MTIA), according to a report from Reuters. Meta previously announced plans to ship a new in-house chip every six months, helping to decrease its reliance on Nvidia and AMD. [Link: Meta to put AI chip into production in September | https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/meta-put-ai-chip-into-production-september-it-looks-double-computing-capacity-2026-07-09/ | Reuters]
